referential_task.1.player.chat_transcrip: [19:00:39] director: Basket 1: a novelty animal basket shaped like a duck/bird in side view—light tan wicker, squat oval body with a top opening, and a head on the right with a small pointed beak and a little blue eye. Let me know when you’ve found/placed it.  [19:01:05] matcher: I placed it!  [19:01:10] director: Basket 2: a round, bucket-shaped woven basket with a thick arched handle that’s very dark brown/black. The body has a tight small weave and a lighter tan band right near the rim. Confirm when placed.  [19:02:05] matcher: I placed it!  [19:02:08] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style basket with a hinged lid that’s open/tilted up. It has a tall central vertical handle rising from the middle, and the lid appears attached on the right side. The weave is light tan. Confirm when placed.  [19:02:26] matcher: Placed!  [19:02:28] director: Basket 4: an oval, shallow basket with a single arched handle; looks like whitewashed wicker with tan accents. The handle has small colored wraps/bands (reddish) near where it meets the sides. Confirm when placed.  [19:03:17] matcher: Are the tan accents on the top of the basket part? like the rim of it I guess I'd say.  [19:03:20] director: Yes—Basket 4 has a lighter/whitish body but a more tan rim/top edge, plus those small reddish wraps on the handle near the sides.  [19:03:33] matcher: Placed it!  [19:03:36] director: Basket 5: a small rectangular basket with a high arched handle made of twisted rope-like wicker. The basket body is darker golden-brown with a chunky square weave; overall looks like a sturdy little market basket. Confirm when placed.  [19:04:52] matcher: Is the chunkier weaved part a slightly different color than the rest?  [19:04:54] director: Yes—on Basket 5 the main body looks a bit darker/denser than the handle, and the weave is chunky like little squares/blocks. The handle is the lighter twisted-rope style.  [19:05:46] matcher: On the handle you can see the weave twisted around it? Its not just straight rope right?  [19:05:49] director: Correct—Basket 5’s handle looks like two strands of wicker twisted together (braided/rope-like), not a smooth straight rod.  [19:06:05] matcher: Placed!  [19:06:08] director: Basket 6: another novelty animal—this one is a round, lidded “cat face” basket viewed from above/front. Light straw color, with a face (eyes, nose, whiskers) on the lid and two little ear points at the top. It sits on small feet. Confirm when placed.  [19:06:43] matcher: Placed!  [19:06:46] director: Basket 7: a rectangular tote basket with a wide arched handle. Distinctive two-tone: the body is dark greenish/teal weave, while the rim and handle are warm orange-brown wicker with a braided band along the top edge. Confirm when placed.  [19:07:03] matcher: Placed!  [19:07:10] director: Basket 8: a squat, oval/rectangular basket with a single handle; overall grey-brown wicker with lighter whitish stakes showing in the weave. Has a darker band around the top rim and a medium-height arched handle. Confirm when placed.  [19:07:45] matcher: Does the weave kind of look thick like sticks in a way?  [19:07:47] director: Yes—Basket 8 has a chunkier “stick-like” weave with visible thicker ribs, and the coloring alternates grey-brown with lighter whitish vertical pieces.  [19:08:22] matcher: Placed!  [19:08:25] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical hamper-like basket, medium brown, with two small loop handles near the top (one on each side). The weave is vertical and it’s noticeably taller than the others—like a basket bin. Confirm when placed.  [19:09:05] matcher: Does it look like it has two handles that are doubled up? Almost like it has 4 handles?  [19:09:07] director: Yes—each side handle on Basket 9 looks like a doubled loop (so it can read like 4 small loops total), sitting right near the top rim.  [19:09:15] matcher: Placed!  [19:09:18] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arched handle. Very light tan, with a spiral coil pattern on the base (concentric rings), and the handle attaches at opposite sides of the round tray. Confirm when placed.  [19:10:03] matcher: Is the inside of the basket and the handle lighter than the outside of the basket?  [19:10:06] director: Yes—Basket 10 has a slightly darker/reddish outer side wall, while the interior base and the handle look lighter straw/tan.  [19:10:16] matcher: Placed!  [19:10:18] director: Basket 11: a deep, round tub basket with no tall handle—just two small side ear-handles. It’s a warm brown wicker with a diagonal chevron-like weave around the sides, and the side handles stick up like short horns. Confirm when placed.  [19:11:21] matcher: Is it bigger than the one you described as a basket bin?  [19:11:23] director: No—Basket 11 is shorter and wider (a tub). Basket 9 is the tallest “bin/hamper” cylinder. Basket 11 just looks chunky and deep with those two small ear-handles.  [19:12:11] matcher: Placed  [19:12:13] director: Basket 12: a medium brown oval basket with a single arched handle, but the rim has two raised peaks/notches—like an “M” shape at the top edges where the handle meets. The sides are more open slatted weave compared to some others. Confirm when placed.  [19:13:01] matcher: Does it have white and tan weave?  [19:13:04] director: No—Basket 12 is pretty uniformly medium brown. The one with more whitish/tan mixed weave is Basket 8. Basket 12’s key cue is that wavy/M-shaped rim with two high points under the handle.  [19:13:38] matcher: The "M" shape, is that all the way around the rim like a design?  [19:13:41] director: It’s mainly at the front/back edges of the rim—two raised points (left and right) with a dip in the middle, giving that “M/wavy” silhouette when you look at the top edge. It’s not a continuous pattern all the way around.  [19:14:42] matcher: Oh, you mean the weave itself looks like an M?  [19:14:44] director: Not the weave pattern—it's the rim outline/shape of the basket opening: the top edge rises into two points (where the handle attaches) with a lower dip between them, making an “M” profile.  [19:15:25] matcher: Placed  [19:15:30] director: Great—basket 1 is the duck/bird-shaped wicker basket in side view: oval body with an open top and a head on the right with a pointed beak and a little blue eye.

[19:17:45] director: Basket 2 is NOT chevron—its weave reads more straight/vertical and banded. The key is it’s a tall cylinder/hamper with small doubled loop handles right at the top rim (can look like 4 little loops total).  [19:18:04] matcher: Placed  [19:18:09] director: Basket 1 (top-left): the light tan rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid propped open and a tall upright handle rising from the center. [19:18:27] director: Basket 3: the round, bowl/bucket-shaped basket with a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the body is brown with a lighter tan band right under the rim. [19:19:06] director: Basket 3’s weave looks mostly tight and horizontal/diagonal around the bowl, not vertical staves. The standout is the very dark thick handle over a rounded bowl body with that lighter band near the top.  [19:19:50] matcher: Is the woven material almost like a stick? Like really thick?  [19:19:53] director: No—Basket 3 is a finer, tight weave (not thick stick-like). The “stick-like/chunky” one is the grey-brown basket we called Basket 8 earlier.  [19:20:16] matcher: And horizontal you mean side to side, not up and down?  [19:20:19] director: Yes—side-to-side bands wrapping around the bowl (not up-and-down vertical ribs).  [19:20:59] matcher: Does the band right before the handle look like it has an arrow like design almost? [19:21:29] director: Basket 4: the small, shallow market basket with a very high arched handle that looks like twisted rope/braided wicker (two strands). [19:22:08] director: Basket 5: the grey-to-blackish woven basket with a medium arched handle and a darker band around the top rim; you can see lighter whitish vertical stakes in the weave (kind of thick stick-like). More boxy/rectangular than the round ones. Confirm when placed.  [19:23:06] matcher: Is the rim, bottom, and handle brown and the body is a greyish green color almost?  [19:23:15] director: Basket 5: yes—handle and rim are brownish/grey-brown, and the body reads grey/charcoal with lighter whitish vertical stakes showing; plus there’s a darker band right at the top rim. [19:25:48] director: Yes—basket 5 is more boxy/rectangular (like a little tote), not a perfect round bowl. [19:26:20] matcher: Does it have red spots at the base of the handle and white spots near the middle/top of the handle?  [19:26:24] director: No—that description (red wraps at the handle base) matches basket 11, the whitish oval basket with tan rim and little reddish wraps. Basket 5 has no red accents; it’s grey/charcoal with whitish vertical stakes and a dark band at the top rim.  [19:27:31] matcher: Does it have a handle?  [19:27:35] director: Yes—basket 5 has a single medium-height arched handle. If you’re not seeing it, it might be getting confused with similar grey/white baskets in your distractors; focus on the dark band right at the top rim plus those whitish vertical stakes.  [19:28:35] matcher: Oh, is it the one with a tan handle, band, and bottom? And the top of the weave right before the rim is white? And between the white and bottom there is a dark piece seperating the two colors?  [19:28:43] director: Yes—if you’re seeing one with a tan-ish handle/rim and a darker strip just under the rim plus some pale/whitish vertical stakes in the side weave, that’s Basket 5. Key check: it has NO red wraps on the handle (those are on basket 11), and it’s more grey/charcoal than the obvious teal-with-orange-trim basket 7.  [19:29:10] matcher: Placed, what is basket 6?  [19:29:17] director: Basket 6: the novelty animal basket shaped like a duck/bird in side view—light tan wicker, oval body with a top opening, and a head on the right with a pointed beak and a small blue eye. Confirm when placed.  [19:29:36] matcher: Placed, what is basket 7?  [19:29:43] director: Basket 7: the rectangular tote with a dark green/teal woven body, but a clearly orange-brown rim and matching orange-brown arched handle. [19:30:13] director: Basket 8: the shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arched handle and a very clear spiral/concentric coil pattern on the base. Or just the rim?  [19:30:51] director: Mostly the whole outer side wall reads that darker reddish-brown, not just the rim. The inside (base spiral) stays lighter.  [19:31:06] matcher: Placed, what is basket 9?  [19:31:14] director: Basket 9: the medium-brown oval basket with a single arched handle, and the top rim has a wavy “M” profile—two raised peaks where the handle meets the sides with a dip in the middle. The side weave is fairly open/slatted. Confirm when placed.  [19:31:36] matcher: It has a uniform color?  [19:31:39] director: Yes—Basket 9 is pretty uniformly medium brown (not mixed white/tan). The main cue is that wavy/M-shaped rim outline under the handle.  [19:31:51] matcher: Placed, what is basket 10?  [19:31:58] director: Basket 10: the deep round tub basket with NO big handle—just two small ear/loop handles on the sides. The side weave is a tight diagonal chevron pattern in medium warm brown. Confirm when placed.  [19:32:46] matcher: There are two baskets, number two and this one, what are the main differences?  [19:32:50] director: Basket 10 (this one): short and wide “tub,” chevron/diagonal weave, and only two small ear-handles on the sides.   Basket 2: tall cylindrical bin/hamper, straighter/vertical-looking weave, and the side handles are small doubled loops right near the top rim.  [19:33:31] matcher: Placed, what is basket 11?  [19:33:39] director: Basket 11: the light/whitewashed oval basket with a tan rim and a single handle that has small reddish wraps where the handle meets the sides. Shallow and pale overall. Confirm when placed.  [19:33:49] matcher: Placed, what is basket 12?  [19:33:57] director: Basket 12: the round, lidded novelty basket that looks like a CAT face—viewed from above, with two little pointed ears, painted eyes/nose/whiskers on the lid, and small feet underneath. Confirm when you’ve placed it.

referential_task.3.player.chat_transcrip: [19:34:36] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a rectangular tote basket with a dark green/teal woven body, but a clearly orange-brown rim and matching orange-brown arched handle; there’s a braided/rope trim running along the top edge. Let me know when you’ve placed it.  [19:35:00] matcher: It's rectangular and the body is tealish?  [19:35:07] director: Yes—Basket 1 is rectangular and the main body is teal/greenish. The giveaway is the orange-brown rim and matching orange-brown handle with a braided trim along the top edge (more contrasty than the greyer baskets).  [19:35:21] matcher: Placed, what is basket 2?  [19:35:28] director: Basket 2: the tall cylindrical hamper/bin basket, medium-brown, open top, with two small side loop handles right near the rim (each looks doubled—can seem like 4 little loops total). Much taller than the other baskets. Confirm when placed.  [19:35:52] matcher: Does it have little windows near the top? it looks like more light is coming in through spots near the top on it  [19:35:59] director: Yes—Basket 2 has those little open “window” gaps near the top rim (more see-through there). It’s the tallest cylindrical bin/hamper, with two small doubled loop handles right up near the rim.  [19:36:12] matcher: Placed, what is basket 3?  [19:36:19] director: Basket 3: the small oval basket with a pale/whitewashed body and a tan rim; it has one arched handle, and there are small reddish wrap/bands right where the handle attaches on both sides. Confirm when placed.  [19:36:54] matcher: There are red wraps, are there also white wraps above the white ones?  [19:36:58] director: On Basket 3, the noticeable wraps are the reddish ones at the handle bases. The handle itself has some lighter sections, but if you’re seeing distinct white wraps/bands up higher on the handle, that might be a different basket—this one’s key cue is *red at the handle attachments* plus the pale body and tan rim.  [19:37:49] matcher: Like actually red wraps or a reddish brown?  [19:37:53] director: More reddish-brown—small dark red/maroon accents, not bright fire-engine red.  [19:38:41] matcher: Is there any white in the weave?  [19:38:45] director: Yes—the basket’s body weave is very light/whitish overall (whitewashed look), with the rim more tan.  [19:39:03] matcher: So in the body, there is white and a light tan?  [19:39:07] director: Exactly—mostly white/very pale straw in the body, with some light tan strands mixed in, and then a clearly tan rim/top edge.  [19:39:25] matcher: But there is no white wraps on the handles?  [19:39:28] director: Right—no distinct “white wrap bands” as a feature; the standout is the reddish-brown wraps at the handle bases.  [19:39:53] matcher: The only one I see with reddish brown wraps at the handle bases also has white wraps near the top of the handle  [19:40:02] director: For basket 3: pick the *very pale/whitewashed* oval basket with a *tan rim*. The key accents are the *small reddish-brown wraps at the two handle attachment points* (where the handle meets the sides). If the handle also has a lighter/whitish section near the top, that’s fine—but the must-have is pale body + tan rim + red-brown wraps at the bases.  [19:40:18] matcher: Placed, what is number 4?  [19:40:22] director: Basket 4: the shallow round tray basket with a single arched handle and a clear spiral/concentric coil pattern on the base. Inside base + handle are lighter straw; the outer side wall is a bit darker reddish-brown. Confirm when placed.  [19:40:52] matcher: Is the outside one uniform color? Or is the rim a different color than the body?  [19:40:56] director: The outside is mostly one darker reddish-brown tone from rim down the side wall—there isn’t a strongly contrasting rim. The contrast is mainly outside darker vs inside/base lighter.  [19:41:06] matcher: Placed  [19:41:13] director: Basket 1: rectangular tote with a dark teal/green body, but a very obvious orange-brown rim and matching orange-brown arched handle; there’s a braided/rope trim along the top edge. [19:41:33] director: Basket 5: the pale/whitewashed oval basket with a tan rim, single arched handle, and small reddish-brown wrap/ties right at BOTH handle attachment points (where the handle meets the sides). [19:43:13] director: Basket 6: the short, wide “tub” basket with two small ear/loop handles on the sides (no big handle). Medium-brown with a tight diagonal chevron/zigzag weave around the body. referential_task.4.player.partner_commen: It was really rough. The first round was great, I only missed two and that was my bad because they were very similar. After round one it just got really confusing. They would describe a basket to me, I would place it, and then later on say the same basket from earlier. The time that happened to me they said to reuse the basket, and then when I said I couldn't, they started changing the whole lineup. I just tried to run through all the baskets again. It felt like after the first round any time I asked questions it just confused them. I don't even know how I got some of them wrong, sometimes I'd get the cat basket or the bunny/duck basket wrong, I have no idea how that happened unless they told me the wrong basket. There are only 2 novelty baskets and they would confirm yes the cat or yes the duck. It was difficult after round 1. Round 3 being the hardest, and then round 4 I was nervous to ask many questions honestly because they had gone so far off the rails prior.
referential_task.4.player.partner_human_: They said the exact same things each time. I could tell by the way they typed, it was very repetitive.
